claude.notes: Claude Code can replace the system prompt via --system-prompt-file when the user owns the session. Reference only; fable ships no Claude executor and does not migrate Claude identity/tool assumptions.

opencode.notes: Executor: assembled prompt passed to `opencode run`. Plus in-session governance via `fable opencode setup` (charter + portable core wired into opencode.json `instructions`, auto-loaded every session; verified vs opencode 1.17.7). Host opencode system prompt remains authoritative; fable overlays governance.

codex.notes: Verified against codex-cli 0.131.0: charter (AGENTS.md) loads fable governance every session, and the fable MCP server (codex mcp add fable) exposes read-only fable_runtime/fable_build_prompt/fable_doctor tools in-session. Host Codex system and tool policy remain authoritative; fable overlays governance.

kimi.notes: Verified against kimi-code 0.14.2: the fable skill (.fable/skills/fable/SKILL.md, loaded via --skills-dir or extra_skill_dirs) carries portable core + kimi adapter governance and is auto-merged each session. Host Kimi system prompt remains authoritative; fable overlays governance.

grok.notes: Verified against grok 0.2.51: the host-agnostic fable MCP server (grok mcp add fable) exposes read-only fable_runtime/fable_build_prompt/fable_doctor in-session, plus charter via AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md. Host Grok system and tool policy remain authoritative; fable overlays governance.

copilot.notes: Verified against GitHub Copilot CLI 1.0.54: the host-agnostic fable MCP server (copilot mcp add fable) exposes read-only fable_runtime/fable_build_prompt/fable_doctor in-session, plus charter via .github/copilot-instructions.md + AGENTS.md. Host Copilot system and tool policy remain authoritative; fable overlays governance.

agy.notes: Generic opaque-host fallback (agy/Antigravity-like). Overlay only; never assume system replacement until proven user-owned and safe.
